EF1 TORNADO OCCURRED IN DECKERVILLE ON LUCAS ROAD JUST SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 63. THIS TORNADO WAS 25 YARDS WIDE AND 250 YARDS LONG. TWO HOMES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND A UTILITY AND PARKING SHED WERE DESTROYED. TREES WERE ALSO REPORTED DOWN.

SEVERAL REPORTS OF DOWNED TRAFFIC SIGNALS AND WIRES FROM DOWNTOWN TO EAST MEMPHIS. REPORTED BY SHELBY COUNTY EMA.
TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL HOUSES...ONE TRAILER DESTROYED...ONE BRICK HOUSE LOST ROOF AND ONE BACK WALL...TREES 24 INCHES IN DIAMETER UPROOTED. NO KNOWN INURIES AT TIME OF REPORT.

INITIAL START POINT OF TORNADO DAMAGE. A MOBILE HOME WAS SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO DAMAGE WAS RATED AS AN EF1 AT THIS LOCATION WITH WINDS BETWEEN 95 MPH AND 105 MPH.

INITIAL START POINT OF TORNADO DAMAGE. A MOBILE HOME WAS SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED. SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO DAMAGE WAS RATED AS AN EF1 AT THIS LOCATION WITH WINDS BETWEEN 95 MPH AND 105 MPH.

TORNADO CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST AND CROSSED DOUBLE BRIDGES ROAD WHERE IT STRUCK TWO HOMES RESULTING IN SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE HOME AND DESTROYED AN OUTBUILDING ON THE SECOND HOME. MULTIPLE LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG WITH DOWNED POWERLINES. THE TORNADO TRACK CONTINUED.

TORNADO CONTINUED ACROSS MILL CREEK TO CHURCH ROAD WHERE THREE HOMES WERE DAMAGED. THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF OF ONE HOME AND COMPLETELY DESTROYED THE KITCHEN AREA. THE OTHER TWO HOMES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE...DAMAGE TO A FENCE...AND A GARAGE COLLAPSED. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AND POWERLINES WERE DOWNED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST.
FARMHOUSE DESTROYED WITH DAMAGE SPREAD OVER 1/2 MILE...MACHINE SHED DESTROYED...TREES DOWN...ALONG HIGHWAY 51 SOUTH OF DYERSBURG

TORNADO REACHED ITS MAXIMUM INTENSITY AS IT MOVED ACROSS HIGHWAY 51 IN THE POPLAR GROVE COMMUNITY. SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO ONE HOME WITH COMPLETE LOSS OF ROOF AND HOUSE SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION. DAMAGE WAS RATED AS A STRONG EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 110 MPH. A MACHINED SHED WAS ALSO DESTROYED. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. POWERLINES WERE DOWNED. THE TORNADO WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 25 YARDS AT THIS LOCATION. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO MOVE QUICKLY TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST.
TORNADO CONTINUED THROUGH WOODED AREA THEN CROSSED HIGHWAY 210. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED.

TORNADO CONTINUED AND CROSSED HOLMES ROAD NEAR FOWLKES COMMUNITY. A HOME SUFFERED MINOR DAMAGE WITH WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND UPSTREAM DEBRIS THROWN INTO TREES. OTHER TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO HAD WEAKENED AND CONTINUED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST.
TORNADO MOVED ACROSS POND CREEK AND ASHLEY ROAD WHERE IT DAMAGED A MOBILE HOME. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST AND THE DAMAGE PATH ENDED.
TRAFFIC LIGHT SIGNALS/WIRES DOWN AT COVINGTON PIKE AND COVINGTON WAY. REPORTED BY SHELBY COUNTY EMA.

THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHDOWN NEAR THE COUNTY LANDFILL WHERE IT BLEW OFF METAL PANELS FROM TWO LARGE SHEDS. THEN IT DOWNED A TREE BEFORE CONTINUING TOWARDS THE NORTHEAST.
A DOZEN LARGE TREES DOWNED. ABOUT FOUR OF THESE TREES EXCEEDED TWO FEET IN DIAMETER. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST.
SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED AND TWISTED. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED IN A MORE EASTERLY DIRECTION.
MINOR DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES. UPROOTED TWO DOZEN LARGE TREES. SNAPPED ADDITIONAL SMALLER TREES. ALL DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROADS 957 AND 992. THE TORNADO THEN LIFTED.

WATER ON HIGHWAY 79.
POWER LINES DOWN ON JAMES MILL ROAD.

*** 1 INJ *** TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY. 385 APPROXIMATELY 1/4 MILE EAST OF BRUNSWICK ROAD AT 500 PM. ONE HOME HAD MODERATE DAMAGE. AT THIS POINT THE TORNADO WAS AN EF0 WITH A WIDTH OF 25 YARDS. THE TORNADO TRAVELLED 6 MILES NON-CONTINUOUSLY. THEN WENT 3 MILES CONTINUOUSLY BETWEEN 504 PM AND 508 PM. THE MAXIMUM INTENSITY WAS ESTIMATED TO BE EF-2 WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS AND RESULTED IN SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES.
TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN TWO 400 FOOT TENNESEE HIGHWAY PATROL/TEMA RADIO TOWERS.
SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF ROOFS.

THIS TORNADO TRAVELED NON-CONTINUOUSLY INTO SOUTHEAST TIPTON COUNTY FROM NORTHEAST SHELBY COUNTY. ONE HOME HAD MINOR DAMAGE ON BEAVER CREEK ROAD AND THERE WAS MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE...DOWNED UTILITY POLES AND TREE DAMAGE AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 59 AND PICKENS STORE ROAD. TORNADO DAMAGE WAS ESTIMATED TO BE EF-0 WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT AROUND 75 MPH. THE TORNADO ENDED IN SOUTHEAST TIPTON COUNTY AT 518 PM.
AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND DAMAGED A FARM BUILDING ROOF AND THEN MOVED NORTHEAST FOR 2 MILES. A PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS DAMAGED AND POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN ALONG NORFOLK ROAD.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN CLARKSDALE.
SHED DAMAGED AND TREES DOWNED
AN EF-0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AND KNOCKED DOWN A FEW TREES IN COURTLAND.

THE FIRST TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED AT SOUTHAVEN HIGH SCHOOL WHERE WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT AND ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION AND AIRWAYS BOULEVARD BEFORE MOVING INTO TENNESSEE AT 534 PM CST.

*** 3 FATAL, 6 INJ *** THIS TORNADO CONTINUED FROM SOUTHAVEN MISSISSIPPI AND PROCEEDED NORTHEAST TO NEAR THE MEMPHIS INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. THE TORNADO STRUCK THE DSC WAREHOUSE CAUSING THE 3 FATALITIES...THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING DAMAGE AT THE HICKORY RIDGE MALL AND 6 ADDITIONAL INJURIES BEFORE LIFTING AT 545 PM CST. THE TOTAL TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS 11 MILES AND WIDTH WAS ONE QUARTER MILE.
ROOF DAMAGE AT FEDEX FIRE STATION AT MEMPHIS INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. 73 KT GUST REPORTED BY WIND SENSOR.
AN EF-0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AND MOVED A MOBILE HOME 2 FEET...DAMAGING THE UNDER PINNINGS.
AN EF-0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN THE COLES POINT COMMUNITY JUST SOUTH OF SARDIS LAKE. THE TORNADO FLIPPED A TRACTOR...DESTROYED A SHED AND CAUSED MINOR TREE DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN IN A WOODED AREA BEFORE DAMAGING A HOME AND OUTBUILDINGS. THE TORNADO MADE MULTIPLE TOUCHDOWNS AS IT MOVED TO THE NORTHEAST.

*** 2 INJ *** DAMAGE STARTED AT INDUSTRIAL AREA...ALONG COUNTY ROAD 1032. SEVERAL INDUSTRIES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED...INCLUDING A CATERPILLAR BUILDING. NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DESTROYED AND DAMAGED. THE PATH WAS ABOUT 8 MILES LONG TOWARD THE NORTHEAST. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
2 HOMES DESTROYED AT PURYEAR.
TORNADO OBSERVED ON GROUND. GIANT TREES BROKE OFF. POWER LINES DOWN NEAR COUNTY ROAD 215 AND COUNTY 291.

*** 1 FATAL *** TORNADO TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED NEAR AKIN ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST. A MAN IN HIS PICKUP TRUCK INSIDE A SHED ON YUM YUM ROAD WAS KILLED AS THE TRUCK WAS FLIPPED AT LEAST 40 FEET. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED IN FAYETTE COUNTY WAS 140 MPH...MAXIMUM WIDTH 500 FEET. TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO HAYWOOD COUNTY AROUND 627 PM CST.
SILVER DOLLAR SIZE HAIL REPORTED.

TORNADO MOVED INTO SOUTHEAST HAYWOOD COUNTY FROM NORTHEAST FAYETTE COUNTY. EF-2 DAMAGE TO DANCYVILLE AND HILLVILLE. MAJOR DAMAGE TO 3 HOUSES...MODERATE DAMAGE TO 1 CHURCH...2 MOBILE HOMES TOTALLY DESTROYED AND CONVENIENCE STORE SUFFERED SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE. LARGE TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. MAXIMUM WINDS 125 MPH...MAXIMUM WIDTH 600 YARDS.
BASEBALL HAIL REPORTED.

*** 2 FATAL *** ENTERED WESTERN MADISON COUNTY...NEAR HWY 138 AND DICKERSON ROAD. TORNADO MOVED THROUGH HUNTERSVILLE AROUND 650 PM CST...CAUSING EF-3 DAMAGE. MAXIMUM WINDS 160 MPH...MAXIMUM WIDTH NEAR 3/4 QUARTERS OF A MILE. WIDESPREAD AND SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES THROUGHOUT HUNTERSVILLE. INTERSTATE 40 REST AREA SUFFERED DAMAGE...8 SEMI-TRAILORS BLOWN OVER. THIS TORNADO TRAVELED CONTINUOUSLY FOR 30 MILES FROM NORTHEAST FAYETTE COUNTY INTO EASTERN MADISON COUNTY.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF CHALYBEATE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 225 AND COUNTY ROAD 222. MANY TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN IN THE AREA. ONE TREE KNOCKED DOWN HALF OF A BRICK CHIMNEY ON A HOUSE LOCATED ON COUNTY ROAD 222-A. A COUPLE OF MOBILE HOMES IN THE AREA WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-0 IN THIS AREA WITH WINDS SPEEDS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 60 MPH. THIS TORNADO THAT WOULD EVENTUAL TRACK NON-CONTINUOUSLY INTO ALCORN AND MCNAIRY COUNTIES.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL IN WALNUT.
GOLF BALL TO BASEBALL SIZE HAIL NORTHWEST OF JACKSON NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF MCCLELLAN AND WINDY CITY ROADS WEST OF THE 45 BYPASS.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AGAIN IN THE COMMUNITY OF UNION CENTER WHICH IS ABOUT ONE MILE WEST OF THE TOWN OF THEO. AT LEAST ONE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED AND SEVERAL OTHER NEARBY MOBILE HOMES WERE ALSO DAMAGED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN IN THE VICINITY. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 IN THE THIS AREA WITH WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 90 MPH. THE WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 100 YARDS.

*** 51 INJ *** EF-4 TORNADO MOVED THROUGH UNION UNIVERSITY IN JACKSON. PEOPLE TRAPPED IN WATER COMMONS BUILDING AT THE UNIVERSITY. OVERTURNED VEHICLES...SEVERE DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS ON CAMPUS AND TO BUILDINGS NEAR THE U.S. 45 HWY BYPASS. DAMAGE AT THE REGIONAL HOSPITAL AT CHANNING WAY DRIVE...DAMAGE AT NORTHSIDE HIGH SCHOOL AND IN THE OAK HILL AREA. MAXIMUM WINDS 170 MPH...MAXIMUM WIDTH 125 YARDS...PATH LENGTH 11 MILES.
QUARTER HAIL REPORTED IN THE OVERLAND HILL COMMUNITY.

THE TORNADO THAT MOVED THROUGH UNION UNIVERSITY CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING EF-3 DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CHRISTMASVILLE ROAD AND ASHPORT ROAD IN NORTHEAST MADISON COUNTY. THE MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 145 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. NUMEROUS HOMES AND A FIRE STATION SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED. THE TOTAL LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO TRACK WAS 11 MILES.
HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL
CAR KNOCKED OFF THE ROAD.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AGAIN EAST OF RAMER KNOCKING DOWN TREES. THE TORNADO THEN TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTVIEW. ONE BUSINESS WAS DESTROYED AND A SECOND BUSINESS WAS DAMAGED ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 45. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST THROUGH A FORESTED AREA WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ACROSS HIGHWAY 142. TWO HOMES RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGED AND ONE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-1 WITH WINDS SPEEDS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 105 MPH AND A WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN SPRING CREEK ALONG HIGHWAY 152 APPROXIMATELY 1/4 MILE WEST OF HIGHWAY 70. ONE HOME SUFFERED A TOTAL ROOF LOSS AND PARTIAL FAILURE OF THE FRONT EXTERIOR WALL. THREE ADDITIONAL HOMES RECEIVED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE. LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 125 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH OF 150 YARDS.
AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 MILES WEST OF COLT.
DAMAGE SURVEY INDICATED A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCH DOWN IN A WOODED AREA ALONG ROGER FRYE ROAD WHICH RESULTED IN DOWNED TREES. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 65 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 25 YARDS.

*** 4 INJ *** 1 HOUSE DESTROYED...5 HOMES WITH MODERATE DAMAGE...1 OUTBUILDING TOTALLY DESTROYED. LARGE METAL BUILDING SEVERLY DAMAGED. 9 MILES NON CONTINUOUS LENGTH STARTING AT 1 WNW DALTON TO NEAR WARM SPRINGS. MAXIMUM WIDTH 200 YARDS. STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THE TORNADO TO BE AN EF-1 WITH WINDS NEAR 110 MILES AN HOUR. INJURIES WERE MINOR.
TREE KNOCKED DOWN ONTO HOUSE. POSSIBLE INJURY.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL

*** 3 FATAL, 5 INJ *** THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED WEST OF THE TENNESSEE RIVER NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COFFEE LANDING ROAD AND TOWBOAT LANE. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO 35 TO 50 HOMES AND NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES OCCURRED AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST. THE THREE DEATHS OCCURRED IN MOBILE HOMES NEAR THE SWIFT COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 114 AND 128 AROUND 757 PM CST. TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 15 MILES AND MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS ONE HALF MILE.

THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF PONTOTOC LAKE A COUPLE OF MINUTES LATER AFTER KNOCKING DOWN A FEW MORE TREES. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 105 MPH...WITH A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 325 YARDS AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 30 YARDS.
